explanations.1: The function has a default parameter 'employees' that will be used as a filename prefix if no argument is provided when calling the function.
explanations.3: The f-string (formatted string) automatically inserts the filename variable into the string, so if filename is 'employees', this loads 'employees_2020.csv'.
explanations.4: The 'inplace=True' parameter modifies the DataFrame directly instead of returning a new one, so the changes stick without needing to reassign.
explanations.15: The 'merge' operation combines two DataFrames side-by-side based on matching values in the 'id', 'first_name', and 'last_name' columns, and 'how='outer'' keeps all rows even if some columns are missing in either file.
